title: pyrus prunocarpa
intro: Pyrus prunocarpa, also known as the wild pear or mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant are rich in tannins and have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus prunocarpa are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues. The plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and infuscatives that can be used to treat a range of ailments.
